project.name: supernode
project.one_liner: Lumera validator companion service for Cascade storage, P2P routing, and Everlight epoch reporting.
project.mission: Provide a Go-based supernode that complements a Lumera validator with Cascade storage/retrieval, Kademlia P2P routing and replication, gRPC/HTTP gateways, and audit-module epoch reporting compatible with Lumera Everlight Phase 1 (chain PR #113).
target_users: Lumera validator operators running a co-located supernode
Devnet/integration test operators driving epoch flows externally
SDK/CLI consumers of cascade storage and challenge evidence
success_metrics: Epoch reports accepted by chain audit module in both prober and non-prober roles without manual intervention
STORAGE_FULL state transitions are driven solely from audit epoch reports and respected by P2P store/write paths
Targeted Go test packages for host_reporter, p2p/kademlia, verifier, and supernode query module pass on master

in_scope: Populating HostReport.cascade_kademlia_db_bytes from local Cascade Kademlia SQLite size in submitted epoch reports
Role-aware peer storage challenge observation submission (prober epoch only)
Supernode query wrappers for Everlight surfaces: pool-state, sn-eligibility, payout-history

P2P Kademlia eligibility policy distinguishing routing/read eligibility (ACTIVE+POSTPONED+STORAGE_FULL) from store/write eligibility (ACTIVE only) for new keys, while preserving existing-key replication and read
Supernode verifier acceptance of STORAGE_FULL as a valid state where appropriate
Test/devnet affordance to disable the on-chain host reporter via LUMERA_SUPERNODE_DISABLE_HOST_REPORTER
LEP-5 availability commitment / Merkle proof challenge support in cascadekit and SDK evidence payload
out_of_scope: Re-implementing legacy supernode_metrics tx path for STORAGE_FULL transitions (superseded by audit epoch reports)
Chain-side (lumera) protocol changes — these are consumed, not redefined here
Coverage threshold enforcement in CI for the retrospectively captured Everlight work
Full repo-wide vet/govulncheck under environments without system WebP development headers
non_goals: Generic operator/admin web UI for Everlight readiness
Backfilling BRIDGE-style acceptance tests for code that pre-dates Everlight and is unrelated to it
Changing existing-key P2P store/replication behavior for STORAGE_FULL self-state
assumptions: Lumera chain at the supported tag exposes audit.v1 SubmitEpochReport with HostReport.cascade_kademlia_db_bytes and the supernode pool-state / sn-eligibility / payout-history queries.
Cascade Kademlia data lives in SQLite files matching data*.sqlite3* under the configured P2P data directory.
Operators install system WebP development headers when they want full repo-wide go test/vet/govulncheck. Build of the supernode binary itself does not require them at run time on release builds.

must_not_use: Legacy supernode_metrics tx path for Everlight payout bytes or STORAGE_FULL state transitions
compliance_security_notes: No secrets in code or logs
Identity must be verified against keyring at host_reporter construction; mismatch is a hard error
Cryptographic correctness of LEP-5 Merkle proof challenge must be preserved

core_entities.name: HostReport
core_entities.notes: audit.v1 host report submitted per epoch. Includes disk_usage_percent, cascade_kademlia_db_bytes (bytes, not MB), and storage_challenge_observations[].

core_entities.name: SupernodeState
core_entities.notes: Enum used by P2P eligibility: ACTIVE, POSTPONED, STORAGE_FULL, plus chain-defined disabled/penalised states. Routing/read eligibility ⊇ store/write eligibility.

core_entities.name: AvailabilityCommitment
core_entities.notes: LEP-5 Merkle commitment over cascade artifact pieces; verified via Merkle proof challenge in cascadekit/SDK evidence path.

state_machine_notes: STORAGE_FULL is reachable from ACTIVE via on-chain state machine driven by audit epoch reports; supernode does not transition itself.
P2P eligibility for self: new-key STORE/BatchStore require ACTIVE; existing-key replication and reads are unchanged.

features.name: Everlight host epoch report payload
features.description: Host reporter populates HostReport.cascade_kademlia_db_bytes from the local Cascade Kademlia SQLite footprint and continues to report disk_usage_percent. Submitted per-epoch via audit module SubmitEpochReport.
features.user_value: Enables Everlight payout weighting and STORAGE_FULL transitions for this validator/supernode pair.

acceptance_tests.criterion: Targeted unit/behavior tests for host_reporter pass and exercise epoch report payload assembly.
Role-aware observation handling: prober epochs include one observation per assigned target; non-prober epochs include none.

features.name: Everlight supernode query wrappers
features.description: Supernode module in pkg/lumera exposes PoolState, SNEligibility, and PayoutHistory wrappers around chain queries, including interface, implementation, mock, and testutil updates.
features.user_value: Lets supernode runtime, operator tooling, and tests obtain payout-readiness data without re-implementing chain query plumbing.

acceptance_tests.criterion: Supernode query module compiles and is exercised via mocks/testutil in dependent packages.

features.name: Everlight STORAGE_FULL P2P policy
features.description: Kademlia eligibility policy treats ACTIVE, POSTPONED, and STORAGE_FULL as routing/read-eligible, but only ACTIVE as store/write-eligible. New-key STORE and BatchStore reject when self is not store-eligible while preserving existing-key replication/read behavior.
features.user_value: Allows STORAGE_FULL nodes to remain useful for retrieval and routing without accepting new writes.

acceptance_tests.criterion: Eligibility helpers and store-rejection paths have executable tests.

features.name: Verifier STORAGE_FULL allowance
features.description: Supernode verifier accepts STORAGE_FULL as a valid state where intended, alongside ACTIVE/POSTPONED, without breaking other verification paths.
features.user_value: Prevents spurious verification failures for nodes that legitimately moved to STORAGE_FULL after an audit epoch.

features.name: Host reporter test/devnet disable affordance
features.description: Setting LUMERA_SUPERNODE_DISABLE_HOST_REPORTER=1 (or true) at startup skips starting the on-chain host reporter, so externally driven devnet epoch-report flows do not race the supernode's own account sequence.
features.user_value: Unblocks deterministic devnet/integration tests that drive epoch reports out-of-band.

acceptance_tests.criterion: Start command honours the env var and the package compiles/tests pass.

features.name: LEP-5 availability commitment support
features.description: Cascadekit exposes commitment helpers and Merkle proof challenge primitives; SDK task evidence aligns its payload with the LEP-5 / no-map chain schema.
features.user_value: Allows the audit pipeline and SDK clients to participate in LEP-5 availability challenges with verifiable Merkle proofs.

acceptance_tests.criterion: Commitment helpers and SDK evidence path have executable tests.

user_flows.name: Per-epoch host report submission
steps: Detect new epoch via chain client
Collect disk_usage_percent and cascade_kademlia_db_bytes from local sources
If prober for this epoch, build StorageChallengeObservations from assigned targets; otherwise none

user_flows.name: P2P new-key store under STORAGE_FULL self-state
steps: Reject when not store-eligible while remaining routable/readable for existing keys

user_flows.name: Devnet test driver disables on-chain host reporter
steps: Drive epoch reports externally without account-sequence collision

reliability: Epoch report submission must be retried at the tx layer when transient errors (e.g. OOG) occur; gas_adjustment is configurable (see commit eae3929).
security: Identity / keyring mismatch at startup is a hard error
No PII or sensitive data in production logs
observability: Successful epoch report submission emits structured log fields. Today: epoch_id, storage_challenge_observations_count. Plan A3 also requested disk_usage_percent, cascade_kademlia_db_bytes, assigned_targets_count — see open_questions and discrepancy D02.

apis.name: audit.v1 SubmitEpochReport
apis.notes: Built and submitted from supernode/host_reporter via pkg/lumera/modules/audit_msg.

external_services.name: Lumera chain RPC
external_services.notes: Source of truth for epoch state, supernode roster, and audit submissions.

integration_acceptance_tests.name: Targeted Everlight test command
integration_acceptance_tests.notes: Smoke command for retrospective Everlight gate; passed on 2026-04-28.

tests.coverage_target: not enforced for retrospective Everlight scope

linters_typechecks: go vet ./... (warns under environments missing system WebP headers)
security_checks: govulncheck ./... (warns under environments missing system WebP headers)

recommended_slices.goal: Retrospectively backfill BRIDGE requirements and gate evidence for completed Everlight compatibility (F01-F06).
exit_criteria: Gate report records remaining discrepancies (D02, D03) without re-opening implementation

recommended_slices.goal: Decide and (if accepted) implement Everlight plan A3 structured log enrichment on host reporter success path.
exit_criteria: Decision recorded: accept or scope-out the additional log fields
If accepted: success log includes disk_usage_percent, cascade_kademlia_db_bytes, observations_count, assigned_targets_count with a unit test

recommended_slices.goal: Decide and (if accepted) implement Everlight plan B2 readiness diagnostics endpoint or CLI command.
exit_criteria: Decision recorded: in-scope or non-goal for this release
If in-scope: new feature(s) added to requirements with their own ATs before any implementation
recommended_slices.notes: Tracks discrepancy D03; if scoped out, move to non_goals.

specialists_recommended.rationale: Future fix slices for these features benefit from explicit test design (role-aware observations, eligibility matrices, env-var gating).
Only relevant if S03 readiness diagnostics endpoint is taken in-scope.
LEP-5 commitment / Merkle proof correctness should be reviewed by a crypto-aware specialist if this area is changed again.

open_questions: Should S02 implement the Everlight plan A3 log fields (disk_usage_percent, cascade_kademlia_db_bytes, observations_count, assigned_targets_count) or should requirements be revised to drop them?
Is the Everlight plan B2 readiness diagnostics endpoint/command in scope for this release? If not, it should be moved from execution.recommended_slices to scope.non_goals.
risks: R1: Wrong units (MB vs bytes) for cascade_kademlia_db_bytes would distort payout — mitigated by F01 tests.
R2: Missing observations for prober epochs would cause rejected reports — mitigated by role-aware tests in F01.
R3: Query wrapper drift against chain proto updates — mitigated by F02 mock/testutil parity.
R4: Operator confusion if STORAGE_FULL nodes appear unreachable when they are actually still routable/readable — mitigated by F03 tests and code comments at selection points.
